mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth signals capacity utilization approaching limits, triggering inflation expectations that compress equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High INDPRO growth correlates with positive 20-day returns during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady payroll employment growth reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across all regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price index exceeds 250.0, elevated inflation triggers immediate risk-off sentiment and portfolio de-risking, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations where CPIAUCSL remains above 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between GDP growth and real sales growth indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall output, forecasting earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP-sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside falling payroll employment confirms a broad labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profits, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent UNRATE increases and PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.
